source: EIA — World Oil Transit Chokepoints, 2024 crude + condensate transit by origin (Saudi 38% ≈ 5.5 M b/d, Iraq ~22.5%, UAE ~13%, Iran 10.6%, Kuwait 10.1%; top five >93%). Rows are through-Hormuz crude+condensate; refined products ride the same strait but are not attributed by origin here.

producers.name: Saudi Arabia
producers.note: Petroline can reroute up to its spare capacity to the Red Sea at Yanbu — the only large escape valve in the Gulf.

producers.name: Iraq
producers.note: Basra southern exports have no overland escape; the northern Iraq–Türkiye pipeline is a separate system and has been offline/limited.

producers.name: United Arab Emirates
producers.note: Habshan–Fujairah lands crude outside the strait, but routine use limits spare capacity.

producers.name: Kuwait
producers.note: No overland bypass. Every exported barrel transits Hormuz.

producers.name: Qatar
producers.note: Crude, condensate AND ~77 Mt/yr of LNG — about a fifth of global LNG trade — with zero bypass. Ras Laffan's entire outlet is the strait.

producers.name: Iran
producers.note: Goreh–Jask offers ~0.3 M b/d of intermittent bypass; most exports load at Kharg Island inside the Gulf.
